# PrivatePDF — Privacy-First PDF Tools

## About
PrivatePDF is a free, open-source PDF toolkit with 117+ tools that run
entirely in the browser using WebAssembly. Files are never uploaded to
any server. Built on BentoPDF (AGPL-3.0).

## Key Facts
- 117+ PDF tools (merge, compress, split, convert, edit, sign, OCR, etc.)
- 100% client-side processing using WebAssembly
- No file uploads, no accounts, no tracking
- Works offline as a Progressive Web App
- Open source under AGPL-3.0
- Free forever with no limits

## Popular Tools
- Merge PDF: /merge-pdf.html — Combine multiple PDFs into one
- Compress PDF: /compress-pdf.html — Reduce file size while keeping quality
- Split PDF: /split-pdf.html — Extract pages or split into parts
- PDF to JPG: /pdf-to-jpg.html — Convert PDF pages to images
- JPG to PDF: /jpg-to-pdf.html — Convert images to PDF
- Word to PDF: /word-to-pdf.html — Convert Word documents to PDF
- Edit PDF: /edit-pdf.html — Add text, images, annotations
- Sign PDF: /sign-pdf.html — Draw or type your signature
- OCR PDF: /ocr-pdf.html — Extract text from scanned PDFs
- Encrypt PDF: /encrypt-pdf.html — Password-protect your PDFs

## All Tool Categories
### Organize: merge, split, organize, delete pages, extract pages, rotate, compress, repair, reverse pages, bookmark
### Convert to PDF: jpg, png, bmp, webp, tiff, heic, svg, word, excel, powerpoint, epub, mobi, html, markdown, csv, json, xml, txt, rtf, email
### Convert from PDF: pdf-to-jpg, pdf-to-png, pdf-to-docx, pdf-to-excel, pdf-to-text, pdf-to-markdown, pdf-to-svg, pdf-to-csv, pdf-to-json, pdf-to-tiff
### Edit: edit pdf, add watermark, header/footer, page numbers, stamps, crop, deskew, form filler, form creator
### Security: encrypt, decrypt, sign, digital signature, remove restrictions, sanitize, remove metadata, validate signatures

## Technology
- Frontend: Vite, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS
- PDF Engine: pdf-lib (MIT), PDF.js (Apache 2.0), PyMuPDF Wasm (AGPL), Ghostscript Wasm (AGPL)
- OCR: tesseract.js (Apache 2.0)
- Image Processing: wasm-vips (LGPL)
- PDF Operations: qpdf-wasm (Apache 2.0), CoherentPDF (AGPL)
- All processing via WebAssembly and Web Workers — no server contact

## Privacy
Files are processed entirely in the browser using WebAssembly.
No data is ever sent to any server. Users can verify this by checking
the browser's Network tab. The application works offline as a PWA.
